question.content: For a rope stretching uniformly from length L at rate v while an ant crawls at speed u relative to the rope, compute the time needed to reach the far end. Output exactly one expression token sequence in plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: a single algebraic expression. Use the variable names specified in the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Use the normalized-coordinate ODE method instead of material-point mapping and ring equivalence.
step_1.key_point: Track the ant by a fraction coordinate along the rope.

step_3.content: Mark the ant by the current rope fraction so the changing length is handled by one position ratio.
step_3.key_point: The fraction from A to the ant is the state variable.

step_5.content: Read the motion as two effects on the same marked point: crawling relative to the rope and rightward drift from uniform stretching.
step_5.key_point: The normalized coordinate turns the two effects into one time-only ODE.

step_7.content: Advance the fraction from its initial value until it reaches the end state 1, which defines the arrival time τ_c.
step_7.key_point: Integrating the fraction law gives the same closed form as the reference answer.
